title: cerastium alpinum subsp. latifolium
intro: Cerastium alpinum subsp. latifolium, also known as the Alpine Mouse-ear or broad-leaved mouse-ear, is a subspecies of the Cerastium alpinum plant, which belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Cerastium alpinum subsp. latifolium include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tea, or used in herbal tinctures and topical creams, making it a versatile addition to natural health remedies.
